# Extracao: transcription_mp4_5__Budjet_Constraints

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_5__Budjet_Constraints.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

---

Budget constraints. How many times have you heard this one? We don't have the money. We don't have the budget. We're out of budget for the quarter. See me, you know, two quarters from now. Look, budgets. What do they even mean? What does the budget even mean? The US government doesn't know what a budget is. Your family probably doesn't know what a budget is. And every company that I have ever met with that said they didn't have the budget, when the problem is big enough, they will find the money. Budget constraints means you have not done a good enough job of finding out the real problem in this company. Because trust me, while that company has a budget constraint, they're spending money on something every day. Electricity, water, gas, utilities, employees, taxes, legal, the list goes on and on and on, right? Budget constraints will always, always mean you haven't done a good enough job of finding out the real problem. Maybe you presented the product, maybe the price is right, they like you, they like your company, your reputation is great, but you have not figured out one thing, the real problem. Find that out and the budget constraint will disappear.
